name: Hip roof
description: Pyramidal four-pitch roof (tejado a cuatro aguas). All four sides slope inward and converge at a single apex, giving uniform overhangs on every facade. Each ascending layer shrinks by one cell on every side; the outer ring uses stairs blocks facing inward and the inner area is filled with roofing material.

required_furniture.rule: Fill inner area of each shrinking layer.
Outer ring on the south side of every layer.
Outer ring on the north side of every layer.
Outer ring on the west side of every layer.
Outer ring on the west side of every layer.
Single solid block (or chimney top) at the convergence point.

placement_rules: Roof footprint must equal the underlying structure footprint.
The base layer of the roof sits at y = aabb.y1 (immediately above the top of the wall AABB); the roof EXTENDS BEYOND the AABB.y1 top.
Hip needs both horizontal dimensions comparable; works best when |W - D| ≤ 2.
Each layer y above the base shrinks by 1 on ALL four sides.
Stairs face inward at each side (north→south, south→north, west→east, east→west).
Total roof height equals floor(min(W, D) / 2)+1 levels including the apex.
On footprints ≥ 8×8 the apex may carry a small chimney top (accent + lantern).
lighting_ventilation: Solid hip roofs do not require windows; ventilation is delegated to the dormer or chimney skills that may pierce this roof.

medieval.notes: Steep, dark wood pitches typical of half-timbered manors.

modern.notes: Shallower visual feel via smooth-stone stairs; lantern apex omitted in minimalist variants.

fantasy.notes: Exaggerated pitch with glowing apex (sea lantern) for tower caps.
